title: ReLU.chat Fragment Metadata Schema
description: Schema for per-fragment metadata consumed by the WASM policy engine. Each fragment in a KB entry's category array becomes an object {text, meta} where meta conforms to this schema.

truth_confidence.description: Factual accuracy / peer-review level. 1.0 = textbook, 0.7 = Wikipedia, 0.5 = LLM-generated, 0.4 = speculative.

style.description: Primary rhetorical style. formal = precise definition/proof; intuitive = plain-language explanation; example = concrete case/illustration; proof = step-by-step derivation; analogy = comparison to familiar concept.

creativity.description: How much the fragment invites creative extension or allows playful variation. 0 = rigid/clinical, 1 = highly open/divergent.

requires.description: Prerequisite entry IDs (or fragment IDs) that should be covered before this fragment is selected. Policy avoids co-selection unless prerequisites already visited.

avoid_with.description: Fragment IDs that are incompatible to co-select with this one. Policy will not include both in a single answer plan.

compatible_with.description: Fragment IDs preferred for co-occurrence. Policy weighs these positively when selecting multiple fragments.

source_confidence.description: Trust in the source material. textbook=1.0, academic_article=0.95, wiki=0.7, LLM-generated=0.4, speculative=0.3.

length_tokens.description: Approximate token count for length-aware selection. Rough heuristic: token count ~= word count * 1.3.
